NKJV.1: A Psalm of David. A Contemplation. Blessed is he whose transgression is forgiven, Whose sin is covered.
NKJV.2: Blessed is the man to whom the LORD does not impute iniquity, And in whose spirit there is no deceit.
NKJV.3: When I kept silent, my bones grew old Through my groaning all the day long.
NKJV.4: For day and night Your hand was heavy upon me; My vitality was turned into the drought of summer. Selah
NKJV.5: I acknowledged my sin to You, And my iniquity I have not hidden. I said, “I will confess my transgressions to the LORD,” And You forgave the iniquity of my sin. Selah
NKJV.6: For this cause everyone who is godly shall pray to You In a time when You may be found; Surely in a flood of great waters They shall not come near him.
NKJV.7: You are my hiding place; You shall preserve me from trouble; You shall surround me with songs of deliverance. Selah
NKJV.8: I will instruct you and teach you in the way you should go; I will guide you with My eye.
NKJV.9: Do not be like the horse or like the mule, Which have no understanding, Which must be harnessed with bit and bridle, Else they will not come near you.
NKJV.10: Many sorrows shall be to the wicked; But he who trusts in the LORD, mercy shall surround him.
NKJV.11: Be glad in the LORD and rejoice, you righteous; And shout for joy, all you upright in heart!

KJV.1: A Psalm of David<S>1732</S>, Maschil<S>4905</S>. Blessed<S>835</S> is he whose transgression<S>6588</S> is forgiven<S>5375</S>, whose sin<S>2401</S> is covered<S>3680</S>. <sup>A Psalm: or, A Psalm of David giving instruction</sup>
KJV.2: Blessed<S>835</S> is the man<S>120</S> unto whom the LORD<S>3068</S> imputeth<S>2803</S> not iniquity<S>5771</S>, and in whose spirit<S>7307</S> there is no guile<S>7423</S>.
KJV.3: When I kept silence<S>2790</S>, my bones<S>6106</S> waxed old<S>1086</S> through my roaring<S>7581</S> all the day<S>3117</S> long.
KJV.4: For day<S>3119</S> and night<S>3915</S> thy hand<S>3027</S> was heavy<S>3513</S> upon me: my moisture<S>3955</S> is turned<S>2015</S> into the drought<S>2725</S> of summer<S>7019</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>.
KJV.5: I acknowledged<S>3045</S> my sin<S>2403</S> unto thee, and mine iniquity<S>5771</S> have I not hid<S>3680</S>. I said<S>559</S>, I will confess<S>3034</S> my transgressions<S>6588</S> unto the LORD<S>3068</S>; and thou forgavest<S>5375</S> the iniquity<S>5771</S> of my sin<S>2403</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>.
KJV.6: For this shall every one that is godly<S>2623</S> pray<S>6419</S> unto thee in a time<S>6256</S> when thou mayest be found<S>4672</S>: surely in the floods<S>7858</S> of great<S>7227</S> waters<S>4325</S> they shall not come nigh<S>5060</S> unto him. <sup>when: Heb. of finding</sup>
KJV.7: Thou art my hiding place<S>5643</S>; thou shalt preserve<S>5341</S> me from trouble<S>6862</S>; thou shalt compass<S>5437</S> me about with songs<S>7438</S> of deliverance<S>6405</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>.
KJV.8: I will instruct<S>7919</S> thee and teach<S>3384</S> thee in the way<S>1870</S> which<S>2098</S> thou shalt go<S>3212</S>: I will guide<S>3289</S> thee with mine eye<S>5869</S>. <sup>guide: Heb. counsel thee, mine eye shall be upon thee</sup>
KJV.9: Be ye not as the horse<S>5483</S>, or as the mule<S>6505</S>, which have no understanding<S>995</S>: whose mouth<S>5716</S> must be held<S>1102</S> in with bit<S>4964</S> and bridle<S>7448</S>, lest<S>1077</S> they come near<S>7126</S> unto thee.
KJV.10: Many<S>7227</S> sorrows<S>4341</S> shall be to the wicked<S>7563</S>: but he that trusteth<S>982</S> in the LORD<S>3068</S>, mercy<S>2617</S> shall compass<S>5437</S> him about.
KJV.11: Be glad<S>8055</S> in the LORD<S>3068</S>, and rejoice<S>1523</S>, ye righteous<S>6662</S>: and shout<S>7442</S> for joy, all ye that are upright<S>3477</S> in heart<S>3820</S>.

ESV.1: Blessed is the one whose transgression is forgiven, whose sin is covered.
ESV.2: Blessed is the man against whom the LORD counts no iniquity, and in whose spirit there is no deceit.
ESV.3: For when I kept silent, my bones wasted away through my groaning all day long.
ESV.4: For day and night your hand was heavy upon me; my strength was dried up as by the heat of summer. Selah
ESV.5: I acknowledged my sin to you, and I did not cover my iniquity; I said, “I will confess my transgressions to the LORD,” and you forgave the iniquity of my sin. Selah
ESV.6: Therefore let everyone who is godly offer prayer to you at a time when you may be found; surely in the rush of great waters, they shall not reach him.
ESV.7: You are a hiding place for me; you preserve me from trouble; you surround me with shouts of deliverance. Selah
ESV.8: I will instruct you and teach you in the way you should go; I will counsel you with my eye upon you.
ESV.9: Be not like a horse or a mule, without understanding, which must be curbed with bit and bridle, or it will not stay near you.
ESV.10: Many are the sorrows of the wicked, but steadfast love surrounds the one who trusts in the LORD.
ESV.11: Be glad in the LORD, and rejoice, O righteous, and shout for joy, all you upright in heart!

NASB.1: [A Psalm] of David. A Maskil. How blessed is he whose transgression is forgiven, Whose sin is covered!
NASB.2: How blessed is the man to whom the LORD does not impute iniquity, And in whose spirit there is no deceit!
NASB.3: When I kept silent [about my sin], my body wasted away Through my groaning all day long.
NASB.4: For day and night Your hand was heavy upon me; My vitality was drained away [as] with the fever heat of summer. Selah.
NASB.5: I acknowledged my sin to You, And my iniquity I did not hide; I said, 'I will confess my transgressions to the LORD'; And You forgave the guilt of my sin. Selah.
NASB.6: Therefore, let everyone who is godly pray to You in a time when You may be found; Surely in a flood of great waters they will not reach him.
NASB.7: You are my hiding place; You preserve me from trouble; You surround me with songs of deliverance. Selah.
NASB.8: I will instruct you and teach you in the way which you should go; I will counsel you with My eye upon you.
NASB.9: Do not be as the horse or as the mule which have no understanding, Whose trappings include bit and bridle to hold them in check, [Otherwise] they will not come near to you.
NASB.10: Many are the sorrows of the wicked, But he who trusts in the LORD, lovingkindness shall surround him.
NASB.11: Be glad in the LORD and rejoice, you righteous ones; And shout for joy, all you who are upright in heart.

NLT.1:
Oh, what joy for those
whose disobedience is forgiven,
whose sin is put out of sight!
NLT.2:
Yes, what joy for those
whose record the LORD has cleared of guilt,
whose lives are lived in complete honesty!
NLT.3:
When I refused to confess my sin,
my body wasted away,
and I groaned all day long.
NLT.4:
Day and night your hand of discipline was heavy on me.
My strength evaporated like water in the summer heat. Interlude
NLT.5:
Finally, I confessed all my sins to you
and stopped trying to hide my guilt.
I said to myself, “I will confess my rebellion to the LORD.”
And you forgave me! All my guilt is gone. Interlude
NLT.6:
Therefore, let all the godly pray to you while there is still time,
that they may not drown in the floodwaters of judgment.
NLT.7:
For you are my hiding place;
you protect me from trouble.
You surround me with songs of victory. Interlude
NLT.8:
The LORD says, “I will guide you along the best pathway for your life.
I will advise you and watch over you.
NLT.9:
Do not be like a senseless horse or mule
that needs a bit and bridle to keep it under control.”
NLT.10:
Many sorrows come to the wicked,
but unfailing love surrounds those who trust the LORD.
NLT.11:
So rejoice in the LORD and be glad, all you who obey him!
Shout for joy, all you whose hearts are pure!

WEB.1: By David. A contemplative psalm. Blessed is he whose disobedience is forgiven, whose sin is covered.
WEB.2: Blessed is the man to whom Yahweh doesn’t impute iniquity, in whose spirit there is no deceit.
WEB.3: When I kept silence, my bones wasted away through my groaning all day long.
WEB.4: For day and night your hand was heavy on me. My strength was sapped in the heat of summer. Selah.
WEB.5: I acknowledged my sin to you. I didn’t hide my iniquity. I said, I will confess my transgressions to Yahweh, and you forgave the iniquity of my sin. Selah.
WEB.6: For this, let everyone who is godly pray to you in a time when you may be found. Surely when the great waters overflow, they shall not reach to him.
WEB.7: You are my hiding place. You will preserve me from trouble. You will surround me with songs of deliverance. Selah.
WEB.8: I will instruct you and teach you in the way which you shall go. I will counsel you with my eye on you.
WEB.9: Don’t be like the horse, or like the mule, which have no understanding, who are controlled by bit and bridle, or else they will not come near to you.
WEB.10: Many sorrows come to the wicked, but loving kindness shall surround him who trusts in Yahweh.
WEB.11: Be glad in Yahweh, and rejoice, you righteous! Shout for joy, all you who are upright in heart!

CSB17.1:
Of David. A . [1] How joyful is the one
whose transgression is forgiven,
whose sin is covered! ⓚ
CSB17.2:
How joyful is a person whom
the LORD does not charge with iniquity ⓛ
and in whose spirit is no deceit! ⓜ
CSB17.3:
When I kept silent, my bones became brittle
from my groaning all day long. ⓝ
CSB17.4:
For day and night your hand was heavy on me; ⓐ
my strength was drained [1]
as in the summer’s heat. ⓑ Selah
CSB17.5:
Then I acknowledged my sin to you
and did not conceal my iniquity.
I said,
“I will confess my transgressions to the LORD,”
and you forgave the guilt of my sin. ⓒ Selah
CSB17.6:
Therefore let everyone who is faithful pray to you immediately. [2] ⓓ
When great floodwaters come,
they will not reach him. ⓔ
CSB17.7:
You are my hiding place; ⓕ
you protect me from trouble. ⓖ
You surround me with joyful shouts of deliverance. Selah
CSB17.8:
I will instruct you and show you the way to go; ⓗ
with my eye on you, I will give counsel. ⓘ
CSB17.9:
Do not be like a horse or mule,
without understanding,
that must be controlled with bit and bridle
or else it will not come near you. ⓙ
CSB17.10:
Many pains come to the wicked, ⓚ
but the one who trusts in the LORD
will have faithful love surrounding him. ⓛ
CSB17.11:
Be glad in the LORD and rejoice,
you righteous ones;
shout for joy,
all you upright in heart. ⓜ

AMP.1:
A Psalm of David. A skillful song, or a didactic or reflective poem. Blessed [fortunate, prosperous, favored by God] is he whose transgression is forgiven,
And whose sin is covered.
AMP.2:
Blessed is the man to whom the LORD does not impute wickedness,
And in whose spirit there is no deceit.
AMP.3:
When I kept silent about my sin, my body wasted away
Through my groaning all the day long.
AMP.4:
For day and night Your hand [of displeasure] was heavy upon me;
My energy (vitality, strength) was drained away as with the burning heat of summer. Selah.
AMP.5:
I acknowledged my sin to You,
And I did not hide my wickedness;
I said, "I will confess [all] my transgressions to the LORD";
And You forgave the guilt of my sin. Selah.
AMP.6:
Therefore, let everyone who is godly pray to You [for forgiveness] in a time when You [are near and] may be found;
Surely when the great waters [of trial and distressing times] overflow they will not reach [the spirit in] him.
AMP.7:
You are my hiding place; You, LORD, protect me from trouble;
You surround me with songs and shouts of deliverance. Selah.
AMP.8:
I will instruct you and teach you in the way you should go;
I will counsel you [who are willing to learn] with My eye upon you.
AMP.9:
Do not be like the horse or like the mule which have no understanding,
Whose trappings include bridle and rein to hold them in check,
Otherwise they will not come near to you.
AMP.10:
Many are the sorrows of the wicked,
But he who trusts in and relies on the LORD shall be surrounded with compassion and lovingkindness.
AMP.11:
Be glad in the LORD and rejoice, you righteous [who actively seek right standing with Him];
Shout for joy, all you upright in heart.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
Count yourself lucky, how happy you must be—
you get a fresh start,
your slate's wiped clean.
MSG.2:
Count yourself lucky—
God holds nothing against you
and you're holding nothing back from him.
MSG.3:
When I kept it all inside,
my bones turned to powder,
my words became daylong groans.
MSG.4:
The pressure never let up;
all the juices of my life dried up.
MSG.5:
Then I let it all out;
I said, "I'll make a clean breast of my failures to God."
Suddenly the pressure was gone—
my guilt dissolved,
my sin disappeared.
MSG.6:
These things add up. Every one of us needs to pray;
when all hell breaks loose and the dam bursts
we'll be on high ground, untouched.
MSG.7:
God's my island hideaway,
keeps danger far from the shore,
throws garlands of hosannas around my neck.
MSG.8:
Let me give you some good advice;
I'm looking you in the eye
and giving it to you straight:
MSG.9:
"Don't be ornery like a horse or mule
that needs bit and bridle
to stay on track."
MSG.10:
God-defiers are always in trouble;
God-affirmers find themselves loved
every time they turn around.
MSG.11:
Celebrate God.
Sing together—everyone!
All you honest hearts, raise the roof!

NIV.1:
Psalm 32
Of David. A maskil.
Blessed is he
whose transgressions are forgiven,
whose sins are covered.
NIV.2:
Blessed is the man
whose sin the Lord does not count against him
and in whose spirit is no deceit.
NIV.3:
When I kept silent,
my bones wasted away
through my groaning all day long.
NIV.4:
For day and night
your hand was heavy upon me;
my strength was sapped
as in the heat of summer. Selah
NIV.5:
Then I acknowledged my sin to you
and did not cover up my iniquity.
I said, “I will confess
my transgressions to the Lord”—
and you forgave
the guilt of my sin. Selah
NIV.6:
Therefore let everyone who is godly pray to you
while you may be found;
surely when the mighty waters rise,
they will not reach him.
NIV.7:
You are my hiding place;
you will protect me from trouble
and surround me with songs of deliverance. Selah
NIV.8:
I will instruct you and teach you in the way you should go;
I will counsel you and watch over you.
NIV.9:
Do not be like the horse or the mule,
which have no understanding
but must be controlled by bit and bridle
or they will not come to you.
NIV.10:
Many are the woes of the wicked,
but the Lord’s unfailing love
surrounds the man who trusts in him.
NIV.11:
Rejoice in the Lord and be glad, you righteous;
sing, all you who are upright in heart!

NRSVCE.1:
Happy are those whose transgression is forgiven,
whose sin is covered.
NRSVCE.2:
Happy are those to whom the LORD imputes no iniquity,
and in whose spirit there is no deceit.
NRSVCE.3:
While I kept silence, my body wasted away
through my groaning all day long.
NRSVCE.4:
For day and night your hand was heavy upon me;
my strength was dried up as by the heat of summer. Selah
NRSVCE.5:
Then I acknowledged my sin to you,
and I did not hide my iniquity;
I said, “I will confess my transgressions to the LORD,”
and you forgave the guilt of my sin. Selah
NRSVCE.6:
Therefore let all who are faithful
offer prayer to you;
at a time of distress, the rush of mighty waters
shall not reach them.
NRSVCE.7:
You are a hiding place for me;
you preserve me from trouble;
you surround me with glad cries of deliverance. Selah
NRSVCE.8:
I will instruct you and teach you the way you should go;
I will counsel you with my eye upon you.
NRSVCE.9:
Do not be like a horse or a mule, without understanding,
whose temper must be curbed with bit and bridle,
else it will not stay near you.
NRSVCE.10:
Many are the torments of the wicked,
but steadfast love surrounds those who trust in the LORD.
NRSVCE.11:
Be glad in the LORD and rejoice, O righteous,
and shout for joy, all you upright in heart.

NET.1: How blessed is the one whose rebellious acts are forgiven, whose sin is pardoned!
NET.2: How blessed is the one whose wrongdoing the LORD does not punish, in whose spirit there is no deceit.
NET.3: When I refused to confess my sin, my whole body wasted away, while I groaned in pain all day long.
NET.4: For day and night you tormented me; you tried to destroy me in the intense heat of summer. (Selah)
NET.5: Then I confessed my sin; I no longer covered up my wrongdoing. I said, “I will confess my rebellious acts to the LORD.” And then you forgave my sins. (Selah)
NET.6: For this reason every one of your faithful followers should pray to you while there is a window of opportunity. Certainly when the surging water rises, it will not reach them.
NET.7: You are my hiding place; you protect me from distress. You surround me with shouts of joy from those celebrating deliverance. (Selah)
NET.8: I will instruct and teach you about how you should live. I will advise you as I look you in the eye.
NET.9: Do not be like an unintelligent horse or mule, which will not obey you unless they are controlled by a bridle and bit.
NET.10: An evil person suffers much pain, but the LORD’s faithfulness overwhelms the one who trusts in him.
NET.11: Rejoice in the LORD and be happy, you who are godly! Shout for joy, all you who are morally upright!
